steps.title: Statement I: The Mean
steps.content: Adding 10 to F gives G with 56 values. Since every value in F is between 150 and 260, and 10 is far below that range, the new mean is pulled down. The mean of G must be less than the mean of F. Statement I is always true.

steps.title: Statement II: The Median
steps.content: F has 55 values; its median is the 28th value. G has 56 values; 10 is the smallest, so sorted G = [10, then all 55 of F sorted]. The median of G = average of G's 28th and 29th values = average of F's 27th and 28th values. If the 27th and 28th values of F are equal (e.g., both 8), the median of G equals the median of F. Statement II is not guaranteed to decrease.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: I only — the mean must decrease; the median need not. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • Choice B: II only — the median is not guaranteed to decrease. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: I and II — Statement II is not always true. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: Neither — Statement I is always true. Eliminated.
